channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How to keep AI hallucinations out of your code
item.description: It turns out androids do dream, and their dreams are often strange. In the early days of generative AI, we got human hands with eight fingers and recipes for making pizza sauce from glue. Now, developers working with AI-assisted coding tools are also finding AI hallucinations in their code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk shared Mars plan over drinks, Star Trek convo: Entrepreneur
item.description: "I realized he was dead serious. Elon Musk doesn't just dream, he makes things happen," said entrepreneur and academic Vivek Wadhwa.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: AMD’s RX 9070 GPUs could go on sale March 6, the day after Nvidia’s RTX 5070 – and I wouldn’t fret about those 900W power supply rumors
item.description: It seems that AMD is planning to unleash its RX 9070 graphics cards in a head-to-head battle with Nvidia’s RTX 5070.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: What is Starlink? How to get the satellite service for free on your phone with T-Mobile, plus everything you need to know
item.description: Starlink's satellite service is starting to hit the mainstream, with T-Mobile now offering it for free on compatible smartphones.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI turns down Musk's takeover bid
item.description: OpenAI’s board of directors has turned down Elon Musk’s $97.4 billion bid to buy the nonprofit that controls the artificial intelligence company. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Unlocking AI’s full potential in procurement with smarter data management
item.description: Supply chain operations are often stymied by inefficiencies that result in higher costs, longer lead times, and dissatisfied customers.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Apple and the big store
item.description: Apple has made a small but significant move by introducing its Apple TV app to Android. It might seem like a minor step, but it marks a major pivot in strategy as the company expands its services beyond its own ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Your new Android notification superpower
item.description: It may seem like a paradox, but notifications are both the best and the worst part of owning an Android device. On the one hand, notifications let us stay on top of important incoming info — be it a critical Slack message, a personal family text, or an email from a high-priority client or colleague.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 8 tips for being a more decisive leader
item.description: When you lead a team, your people will turn to you to make the final call on a huge number of decisions. People admire a leader who is decisive. But decisiveness is challenging to master. You have to live with the decisions you make.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Grupo Piñero optimiza la experiencia del cliente a nivel tecnológico, operativo y participativo
item.description: Grupo Piñero optimiza la experiencia del cliente a nivel tecnológico, operativo y participativo. Se apoya para ello en la plataforma unificada de gestión de la experiencia del cliente de Sprinklr.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Buckle up for faster Python programs
item.description: In this edition of our biweekly Python newsletter: The next version of Python brings an all-new variety of speed boost. Python packages may soon pack better labeling for their ingredients. Cython 3.1 tees up new features for converting Python to C.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple has been charged with abuse of power by this German watchdog. Here’s why
item.description: The German antitrust authority has charged Apple with abusing its market power through its app tracking tool and giving itself preferential treatment in a move that could result in daily fines for the iPhone maker if it fails to change its business practices.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: SpaceX’s Starbase site inches closer to being its own city in Texas
item.description: A Texas county on Wednesday approved holding an election sought by SpaceX that would let residents living around billionaire Elon Musk’s company decide whether to formally create a new city called Starbase.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Bristling at 'Gulf of Mexico' name change on maps, Mexico says it might sue Google
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum says her government is not ruling out filing a civil lawsuit against Google if it maintains its stance of calling the stretch of sea between northeastern Mexico and the southeastern United States the “Gulf of America.”.
item.dc:creator: Fabiola Sânchez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Instagram’s AI bots are often sexually suggestive—and sometimes underage
item.description: When Meta launched its “AI Studio” feature for over two billion Instagram users in July 2024, the company promised a tool that would give anyone the ability to create their own AI characters “to make you laugh, generate memes, give travel advice, and so much more.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: IP Fabric expands network visibility, automation with platform update
item.description: As modern networking deployments across the cloud and edge become increasingly complex, it can be a non-trivial exercise for teams to get proper visibility.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to Handle Fake Red Light Camera Tickets
item.description: Learn how to spot fake red light camera tickets, what to do if you receive one, and your options when faced with a genuine ticket but not the driver.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
